title: Contributing to bgms
text: Contributions come many forms, valued: Report bug — open issue bug report template load automatically. Suggest feature — open issue feature request template load automatically. Improve documentation — fix typo, clarify vignette, add example. Write tests — help us cover edge cases new functionality. Review pull request — second pair eyes always welcome. Contribute code — fix bug, implement feature, improve performance. small changes (typos, documentation fixes), open pull request directly. larger work (new features, algorithm changes), open issue first can discuss approach invest time.

title: Model Comparison with bgmCompare
text: function bgmCompare() extends bgm() independent-sample designs. estimates whether edge weights category thresholds differ across groups ordinal Markov random field (MRF). Posterior inclusion probabilities indicate plausible group difference exists given parameter. can converted Bayes factors hypothesis testing.

title: Model Comparison with bgmCompare
text: illustrate subset ADHD dataset included bgms.
code: library(bgms)  ?ADHD data_adhd = ADHD[ADHD$group == 1, -1] data_adhd = data_adhd[, 1:5] data_no_adhd = ADHD[ADHD$group == 0, -1] data_no_adhd = data_no_adhd[, 1:5]

title: Model Comparison with bgmCompare
code: fit = bgmCompare(x = data_adhd, y = data_no_adhd, seed = 1234)

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: quality Markov chain can assessed common MCMC diagnostics: R-hat values close 1 (typically 1.01) suggest convergence (Vehtari et al., 2021). effective sample size (ESS) reflects number independent samples provide equivalent precision. Larger ESS values indicate reliable estimates. Monte Carlo standard error (MCSE) measures additional variability introduced using finite number MCMC draws. small MCSE relative posterior standard deviation indicates stable estimates, whereas large MCSE suggests samples needed.

what: Two ESS measures for edge-selected parameters
title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: edge difference selection active, effect parameters governed spike--slab priors. corresponding parameter set exactly zero effect excluded, rather removed model. parameter well-defined value every iteration, full chain — including zeros — valid sequence computing ESS. n_eff unconditional ESS, computed full effect chain. measures precisely overall posterior mean estimated. n_eff_mixt mixture ESS. measures precisely posterior mean effect estimated accounting additional uncertainty introduced spike--slab selection. indicator rarely switches inclusion exclusion (fewer 5 transitions), n_eff_mixt suppressed printed output.

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: Users can inspect traceplots extracting raw samples directly. example pairwise effect parameter.
code: param_index = 1 chains = fit$raw_samples$pairwise nchains = length(chains) cols = c("firebrick", "steelblue", "darkgreen", "goldenrod")  plot(chains[[1]][, param_index],   type = "l", col = cols[1],   xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "Value",   main = "Traceplot of pairwise[1]",   ylim = range(sapply(chains, function(ch) range(ch[, param_index]))) ) if(nchains > 1) {   for(c in 2:nchains) {     lines(chains[[c]][, param_index], col = cols[c])   } }

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: spike--slab prior yields posterior inclusion probabilities edges: Values near 1.0: strong evidence edge present. Values near 0.0: strong evidence edge absent. Values near 0.5: inconclusive (absence evidence).

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: prior inclusion probability edge equal 0.5 (e.g., using Bernoulli prior inclusion_probability = 0.5 symmetric Beta prior, main_alpha = main_beta), can directly transform inclusion probabilities Bayes factors edge presence vs absence: Bayes factor favor inclusion (H1) small, meaning little evidence inclusion. Since Bayes factor transitive, can use express evidence favor exclusion (H0) Bayes factor shows strong evidence absence network relation variables intrusion physior.
code: # Example for one edge p = coef(fit)$indicator[1, 5] BF_10 = p / (1 - p) BF_10 #> [1] 25.31579 1 / BF_10 #> [1] 0.03950104

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: using update_method = "nuts" (default), additional diagnostics available assess quality Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampling. can accessed via fit$nuts_diag:

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: E-BFMI (Energy Bayesian Fraction Missing Information) measures efficiently sampler explores posterior. compares typical size energy changes successive samples overall spread energies. Values close 1 indicate sampler moves freely across energy landscape; values 0.3 suggest sampler may getting stuck chain yet settled stationary distribution. low E-BFMI necessarily mean results wrong, warrant investigation. models edge selection, common cause warmup period short discrete graph structure equilibrate. Increasing warmup often resolves .

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: Divergent transitions occur numerical integrator encounters regions posterior curvature changes rapidly current step size. small number divergences (say, fewer 0.1% samples) generally acceptable. However, many divergences indicate sampler may missing important parts posterior. see large number divergences, consider increasing target_accept (makes sampler use smaller step size) , fix , switching update_method = "adaptive-metropolis".

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: NUTS builds trajectories repeatedly doubling length “U-turn” criterion satisfied. trajectory frequently reaches maximum allowed depth (nuts_max_depth, default 10), suggests sampler may benefit longer trajectories explore posterior efficiently. Hitting maximum depth occasionally normal; hitting iterations may indicate challenging posterior geometry. happens, consider increasing nuts_max_depth.

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: MRFs continuous variables, leapfrog integrator enforces equality constraints projection step. forward step, integrator checks whether reversing step returns starting point. round-trip error exceeds tolerance scaled square step size, step flagged non-reversible. small number non-reversible steps concern. large number indicates step size large constraint geometry. step size tuned warmup, effective remedy increase warmup adapter time find appropriate step size. non-reversible steps persist increasing warmup, switch update_method = "adaptive-metropolis".

title: Diagnostics and Spike-and-Slab Summaries
text: Standard HMC/NUTS warmup designed tune step size mass matrix continuous parameters. models edge selection, discrete graph structure may take longer reach stationary distribution continuous parameters. result, even warmup completes, first portion sampling phase may still show transient behavior (.e., non-stationarity). warmup_check component provides simple diagnostics compare first second halves post-warmup samples: returned list contains following fields (one value per chain): warmup_incomplete: logical flag TRUE indicators suggest chain may reached stationarity. energy_slope: slope linear regression energy iteration number. slope near zero indicates stable energy; significant negative slope suggests chain still drifting toward higher-probability regions. slope_significant: TRUE energy slope statistically significant (p < 0.01). ebfmi_first_half ebfmi_second_half: E-BFMI computed separately first second halves post-warmup samples. first-half value much lower (example, 0.3) second-half value healthy, early samples likely still settling. var_ratio: ratio energy variance first half second half. ratio much greater 1 (example, 2) indicates higher variability early , consistent transient behavior. diagnostics suggest chain still settling, increase warmup re-run model. diagnostics remain problematic substantial increase (example, doubling tripling warmup), consider re-fitting update_method = "adaptive-metropolis" comparing posterior summaries. two samplers produce similar results, estimates likely trustworthy despite warnings; differ substantially, warrants investigation model data.

title: Getting Started with bgms
text: bgms package implements Bayesian methods analyzing graphical models. supports three variable types: ordinal (including binary) — Markov random field (MRF) models, Blume–Capel — ordinal MRF reference category, continuous — Gaussian graphical models (GGM). package estimates main effects pairwise interactions, optional Bayesian edge selection via spike--slab priors. provides two main entry points: bgm() one-sample designs (single network), bgmCompare() independent-sample designs (group comparisons). vignette walks basic workflow ordinal data. continuous data, set variable_type = "continuous" bgm() fit Gaussian graphical model.

what: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
title: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
text: bgm function estimates pseudoposterior distribution parameters Markov Random Field (MRF) binary, ordinal, continuous, mixed (discrete continuous) variables. Depending variable types, model ordinal MRF, Gaussian graphical model (GGM), mixed MRF. Optionally, performs Bayesian edge selection using spike--slab priors infer network structure.

title: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
code: bgm(   x,   variable_type = "ordinal",   baseline_category,   iter = 1000,   warmup = 1000,   interaction_prior = cauchy_prior(scale = 1),   threshold_prior = beta_prime_prior(alpha = 0.5, beta = 0.5),   means_prior = normal_prior(scale = 1),   precision_scale_prior = gamma_prior(shape = 1, rate = 1),   edge_selection = TRUE,   edge_prior = bernoulli_prior(0.5),   na_action = c("listwise", "impute"),   update_method = c("nuts", "adaptive-metropolis"),   target_accept,   nuts_max_depth = 10,   learn_mass_matrix = TRUE,   chains = 4,   cores = parallel::detectCores(),   display_progress = c("per-chain", "total", "none"),   seed = NULL,   standardize = FALSE,   verbose = getOption("bgms.verbose", TRUE),   progress_callback = NULL,   pairwise_scale,   main_alpha,   main_beta,   inclusion_probability,   beta_bernoulli_alpha,   beta_bernoulli_beta,   beta_bernoulli_alpha_between,   beta_bernoulli_beta_between,   dirichlet_alpha,   lambda,   interaction_scale,   burnin,   save,   threshold_alpha,   threshold_beta )

title: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
text: x data frame matrix n rows p columns. Columns may contain binary, ordinal, continuous variables (see variable_type). Discrete variables automatically recoded non-negative integers (0, 1, ..., m); regular ordinal variables, unobserved categories collapsed, Blume–Capel variables retain categories. Continuous variables column-centered internally GGM likelihood formulated zero-mean assumption. variable_type Character character vector. Specifies type variable x. Allowed values: "ordinal", "blume-capel", "continuous". single string applies variables. per-variable vector mixes discrete ("ordinal" / "blume-capel") "continuous" types fits mixed MRF. Binary variables automatically treated "ordinal". Default: "ordinal". baseline_category Integer vector. Baseline category used Blume–Capel variables. Can single integer (applied ) vector length p. Required least one variable type "blume-capel". iter Integer. Number post–burn-iterations (per chain). Default: 1e3. warmup Integer. Number warmup iterations collecting samples. minimum 1000 iterations enforced, warning smaller value requested. Default: 1e3. interaction_prior prior specification object pairwise interaction parameters, created one prior constructor functions: cauchy_prior(): Cauchy(0, scale) prior (default). normal_prior(): Normal(0, scale) prior. beta_prime_prior(): Beta-prime prior. Default: cauchy_prior(scale = 1). threshold_prior prior specification object threshold (main effect) parameters, created one prior constructor functions: beta_prime_prior(): Beta-prime prior (default). cauchy_prior(): Cauchy(0, scale) prior. normal_prior(): Normal(0, scale) prior. Default: beta_prime_prior(alpha = 0.5, beta = 0.5). means_prior prior specification object continuous variable means (mixed MRF models ), created one prior constructor functions: normal_prior(): Normal(0, scale) prior (default). cauchy_prior(): Cauchy(0, scale) prior. beta_prime_prior(): Beta-prime prior. used model includes continuous variables. Ignored pure ordinal pure continuous (GGM) models. Default: normal_prior(scale = 1). precision_scale_prior prior specification object diagonal elements precision matrix, created one : gamma_prior(): Gamma(shape, rate) prior (default). exponential_prior(): Exponential(rate) prior. used models continuous variables (GGM mixed MRF). Ignored pure ordinal models. Default: gamma_prior(shape = 1, rate = 1). edge_selection Logical. Whether perform Bayesian edge selection. FALSE, model estimates edges. Default: TRUE. edge_prior edge prior specification object, character string (deprecated). Specifies prior edge inclusion. Preferred: pass object one : bernoulli_prior(): Fixed inclusion probability (default). beta_bernoulli_prior(): Beta-distributed inclusion. sbm_prior(): Stochastic Block Model. Legacy character strings "Bernoulli", "Beta-Bernoulli", "Stochastic-Block" still accepted deprecated. Default: bernoulli_prior(0.5). na_action Character. Specifies missing data handling. Either "listwise" (drop rows missing values) "impute" (perform single imputation sampling). Default: "listwise". update_method Character. Specifies MCMC sampler updates model parameters: "adaptive-metropolis" Componentwise adaptive Metropolis–Hastings Robbins–Monro proposal adaptation. "nuts" -U-Turn Sampler RATTLE constrained integration Gaussian models edge selection. Default: "nuts". target_accept Numeric 0 1. Target acceptance rate sampler. Defaults set automatically supplied: 0.44 adaptive Metropolis 0.80 NUTS. nuts_max_depth Integer. Maximum tree depth NUTS. Must positive. Default: 10. learn_mass_matrix Logical. TRUE, adapt diagonal mass matrix warmup (NUTS ). FALSE, use identity matrix. Default: TRUE. chains Integer. Number parallel chains run. Default: 4. cores Integer. Number CPU cores parallel execution. Default: parallel::detectCores(). display_progress Character. Controls progress reporting sampling. Options: "per-chain" (separate bar per chain), "total" (single combined bar), "none" (progress). Default: "per-chain". seed Optional integer. Random seed reproducibility. Must single non-negative integer. standardize Logical. TRUE, prior scale pairwise interaction adjusted based range response scores. Variables response categories larger score products \(x_i \cdot x_j\), typically correspond smaller interaction effects \(\sigma_{ij}\). Without standardization, fixed prior scale relatively wide smaller effects, resulting less shrinkage high-category pairs shrinkage low-category pairs. Standardization scales prior proportionally maximum score product, ensuring equivalent relative shrinkage across pairs. internal recoding, regular ordinal variables scores \(0, 1, \ldots, m\). adjusted scale interaction variables \(\) \(j\) pairwise_scale * m_i * m_j, pairwise_scale applies unit interval case (binary variables \(m_i = m_j = 1\)). Blume-Capel variables reference category \(b\), scores centered \(-b, \ldots, m-b\), adjustment uses maximum absolute product score endpoints. mixed pairs, ordinal variables use raw score endpoints \((0, m)\) Blume-Capel variables use centered score endpoints \((-b, m-b)\). Default: FALSE. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints informational messages data processing (e.g., missing data handling, variable recoding). Defaults getOption("bgms.verbose", TRUE). Set options(bgms.verbose = FALSE) suppress messages globally. progress_callback optional R function signature function(completed, total) called regular intervals sampling, completed number iterations completed across chains total total number iterations. Useful external front-ends (e.g., JASP) supply progress reporting. NULL (default), callback invoked. pairwise_scale Double. Scale Cauchy prior pairwise interaction parameters. Use interaction_prior instead. Default: 1. main_alpha, main_beta Double. Shape parameters beta-prime prior threshold parameters. Use threshold_prior instead. Defaults: main_alpha = 0.5 main_beta = 0.5. inclusion_probability Numeric scalar. Use edge_prior = bernoulli_prior(inclusion_probability) instead. Default: 0.5. beta_bernoulli_alpha, beta_bernoulli_beta Double. Use edge_prior = beta_bernoulli_prior(alpha, beta) instead. Defaults: 1. beta_bernoulli_alpha_between, beta_bernoulli_beta_between Double. Use edge_prior = sbm_prior(alpha_between, beta_between) instead. Defaults: 1. dirichlet_alpha Double. Use edge_prior = sbm_prior(dirichlet_alpha = ...) instead. Default: 1. lambda Double. Use edge_prior = sbm_prior(lambda = ...) instead. Default: 1. interaction_scale, burnin, save, threshold_alpha, threshold_beta Deprecated arguments bgms 0.1.6.0. Use pairwise_scale, warmup, main_alpha, main_beta instead.

title: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
text: list class "bgms" posterior summaries, posterior mean matrices, access raw MCMC draws. object can passed print(), summary(), coef(). Main components include: posterior_summary_main: Data frame posterior summaries (mean, sd, MCSE, ESS, Rhat) main-effect parameters. OMRF models category thresholds; mixed MRF models discrete thresholds continuous means. NULL GGM models (main effects). posterior_summary_quadratic: Data frame posterior summaries residual variance parameters (GGM mixed MRF). NULL OMRF models. posterior_summary_pairwise: Data frame posterior summaries partial association parameters. posterior_summary_indicator: Data frame posterior summaries edge inclusion indicators (edge_selection = TRUE). posterior_mean_main: Posterior mean main-effect parameters. NULL GGM models. OMRF: matrix (p x max_categories) category thresholds. mixed MRF: list $discrete (threshold matrix) $continuous (q x 1 matrix means). posterior_mean_pairwise: Symmetric matrix posterior mean partial associations (zero diagonal). continuous variables unstandardized partial correlations; discrete variables half log adjacent-category odds ratio. Use extract_precision(), extract_partial_correlations(), extract_log_odds() convert interpretable scales. posterior_mean_residual_variance: Named numeric vector posterior mean residual variances \(1/\Theta_{ii}\). Present GGM mixed MRF models; NULL OMRF. posterior_mean_indicator: Symmetric matrix posterior mean inclusion probabilities (edge selection enabled). Additional summaries returned edge_prior = "Stochastic-Block". details prior see Sekulovski et al. (2025) . posterior_summary_pairwise_allocations: Data frame posterior summaries (mean, sd, MCSE, ESS, Rhat) pairwise cluster co-occurrence nodes. serves indicate whether estimated posterior allocations,co-clustering matrix posterior cluster probabilities (see blow) converged. posterior_coclustering_matrix: symmetric matrix pairwise proportions occurrence every variable. matrix can plotted visually inspect estimated number clusters visually inspect nodes tend switch clusters. posterior_mean_allocations: vector posterior mean cluster allocations nodes. calculated using method proposed Dahl (2009) . posterior_mode_allocations: vector posterior mode cluster allocations nodes. posterior_num_blocks: data frame estimated posterior inclusion probabilities possible number clusters. raw_samples: list raw MCMC draws per chain: main List main effect samples. pairwise List pairwise effect samples. indicator List indicator samples (edge selection enabled). allocations List cluster allocations (SBM prior used). nchains Number chains. niter Number post–warmup iterations per chain. parameter_names Named lists parameter labels. arguments: list function call arguments metadata (e.g., number variables, warmup, sampler settings, package version). summary() method prints formatted posterior summaries, coef() extracts posterior mean matrices. NUTS diagnostics (tree depth, divergences, energy, E-BFMI) included fit$nuts_diag update_method = "nuts".

title: Bayesian Estimation or Edge Selection for Markov Random Fields — bgm
text: Depending variable types, model ordinal MRF, Gaussian graphical model (GGM), mixed MRF. regular ordinal variables Blume–Capel ordinal variables (baseline category) supported. Edge selection uses spike--slab priors Bernoulli, Beta-Bernoulli, Stochastic-Block inclusion priors. Parameters sampled NUTS (default) adaptive Metropolis–Hastings, multi-stage warmup schedule. Missing data can handled via listwise deletion Gibbs imputation. full details model specification, prior choices, warmup, output interpretation, see package website https://bayesian-graphical-modelling-lab.github.io/bgms-docs/.

FALSE, main effect differences always included (selection). Since main effects often nuisance parameters selection can interfere pairwise selection Beta-Bernoulli prior, default FALSE. used difference_selection = TRUE. variable_type Character vector specifying type variable: "ordinal" (default) "blume-capel". baseline_category Integer vector giving baseline category Blume–Capel variables. difference_scale Double. Scale Cauchy prior difference parameters. Default: 1. difference_prior indicator prior specification object difference selection, created one : bernoulli_prior(): Fixed inclusion probability (default). beta_bernoulli_prior(): Beta-distributed inclusion. sbm_prior(): Stochastic Block Model. Legacy character strings "Bernoulli" "Beta-Bernoulli" still accepted deprecated. Default: bernoulli_prior(0.5). difference_probability Numeric. Use difference_prior = bernoulli_prior(probability) instead. Default: 0.5. interaction_prior prior specification object baseline pairwise interaction parameters, created one prior constructor functions: cauchy_prior(): Cauchy(0, scale) prior (default). normal_prior(): Normal(0, scale) prior. supplied, overrides pairwise_scale. Default: cauchy_prior(scale = 1). threshold_prior prior specification object threshold (main effect) parameters, created one prior constructor functions: beta_prime_prior(): Beta-prime prior (default). normal_prior(): Normal(0, scale) prior. supplied, overrides main_alpha main_beta. Default: beta_prime_prior(alpha = 0.5, beta = 0.5). iter Integer. Number post–warmup iterations per chain. Default: 1e3. warmup Integer. Number warmup iterations sampling. Default: 1e3. na_action Character. handle missing data: "listwise" (drop rows) "impute" (impute within Gibbs). Default: "listwise". update_method Character. Sampling algorithm: "adaptive-metropolis" "nuts". Default: "nuts". target_accept Numeric 0 1. Target acceptance rate. Defaults: 0.44 (Metropolis), 0.80 (NUTS). nuts_max_depth Integer. Maximum tree depth NUTS. Default: 10. learn_mass_matrix Logical. TRUE, adapts diagonal mass matrix warmup (NUTS ). Default: TRUE. chains Integer. Number parallel chains. Default: 4. cores Integer. Number CPU cores. Default: parallel::detectCores(). display_progress Character. Controls progress reporting: "per-chain", "total", "none". Default: "per-chain". seed Optional integer. Random seed reproducibility. standardize Logical. TRUE, Cauchy prior scale pairwise interaction (baseline difference) adjusted based range response scores. Without standardization, pairs response categories experience less shrinkage naturally smaller interaction effects make fixed prior relatively wide. TRUE, prints informational messages data processing (e.g., missing data handling, variable recoding). Defaults getOption("bgms.verbose", TRUE). Set options(bgms.verbose = FALSE) suppress messages globally. progress_callback optional R function signature function(completed, total) called regular intervals sampling, completed number iterations completed across chains total total number iterations. what: bgms: Bayesian Analysis of Graphical Models — bgms-package
title: bgms: Bayesian Analysis of Graphical Models — bgms-package
text: R package bgms provides tools Bayesian analysis graphical models describing networks binary, ordinal, continuous, mixed variables (Marsman et al. 2025) . Supported model families include ordinal Markov random fields (MRFs), Gaussian graphical models (GGMs), mixed MRFs combine discrete continuous variables single network. likelihood approximated via pseudolikelihood, Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) methods used sample corresponding pseudoposterior distribution model parameters. main entry points : bgm: estimation one-sample design. Use variable_type = "ordinal" MRF, "continuous" GGM, per-variable vector mixing "ordinal", "blume-capel", "continuous" mixed MRF. bgmCompare: estimation group comparison independent-sample design. functions support Bayesian effect selection spike--slab priors. one-sample designs, bgm models presence absence edges variables. Posterior inclusion probabilities quantify plausibility edge can converted Bayes factors conditional independence tests. bgm can also model communities (clusters) variables. posterior distribution number clusters provides evidence clustering (Sekulovski et al. 2025) . independent-sample designs, bgmCompare estimates group differences edge weights category thresholds. Posterior inclusion probabilities quantify evidence differences can converted Bayes factors parameter equivalence tests (Marsman et al. 2025) .

title: Predict Conditional Probabilities from a Fitted bgms Model — predict.bgms
text: Ordinal models: type = "probabilities": named list one element per predicted variable. element matrix n rows num_categories + 1 columns containing \(P(X_j = c | X_{-j})\) observation category. type = "response": matrix n rows length(variables) columns containing predicted categories. method = "posterior-sample", probabilities averaged posterior draws, attribute "sd" included containing standard deviation across draws. GGM (continuous) models: type = "probabilities": named list one element per predicted variable. element matrix n rows 2 columns ("mean" "sd") containing conditional Gaussian parameters \(E(X_j | X_{-j})\) \(\text{SD}(X_j | X_{-j})\). type = "response": matrix n rows length(variables) columns containing conditional means. method = "posterior-sample", conditional parameters averaged posterior draws, attribute "sd" included. Mixed MRF models: mixed models, return list contains elements discrete continuous predicted variables. Discrete variables return probability matrices (ordinal models); continuous variables return conditional mean SD matrices (GGM models).

title: Predict Conditional Probabilities from a Fitted bgms Model — predict.bgms
text: observation, function computes conditional distribution target variable(s) given observed values variables. conditional distribution used internally Gibbs sampler. GGM (continuous) models, conditional distribution \(X_j | X_{-j}\) Gaussian mean \(-\omega_{jj}^{-1} \sum_{k \neq j} \omega_{jk} x_k\) variance \(\omega_{jj}^{-1}\), \(\Omega\) precision matrix.

K_diag Numeric matrix size n_samples x p containing diagonal entries \(K_{11}, \ldots, K_{pp}\). offdiag_names Character vector length p * (p - 1) / 2 naming columns K_offdiag (e.g. "K_1_2"). diag_names Character vector length p naming columns K_diag. step_size (initial) NUTS step size used. edge_indicators integer edge-indicator matrix used (full graph supplied).

title: Simulate Observations from a Markov Random Field — simulate_mrf
text: num_states number observations generated. num_variables number variables MRF. num_categories Either positive integer vector positive integers length num_variables. number response categories top base category: num_categories = 1 generates binary states. used ordinal Blume-Capel variables; ignored variable_type = "continuous". pairwise symmetric num_variables num_variables matrix. ordinal Blume-Capel variables, contains pairwise interaction parameters; -diagonal elements used. continuous variables, precision matrix \(\Omega\) (including diagonal) must positive definite. main ordinal Blume-Capel variables: num_variables max(num_categories) matrix category thresholds. elements row indicate thresholds variable . num_categories vector, first num_categories[] elements used row . Blume-Capel model used category thresholds variable , row requires two values (details ); first \(\alpha\), linear contribution Blume-Capel model second \(\beta\), quadratic contribution. continuous variables: numeric vector length num_variables containing means \(\mu\) variable. Defaults zeros supplied values zero. variable_type kind variables simulated? Can single character string specifying variable type p variables vector character strings length p specifying type variable separately. Currently, bgm supports "ordinal", "blume-capel", "continuous". Binary variables automatically treated "ordinal". Ordinal Blume-Capel variables can mixed freely, continuous variables mixed ordinal Blume-Capel variables. variable_type = "continuous", function simulates Gaussian graphical model. Defaults variable_type = "ordinal". baseline_category integer vector length num_variables specifying baseline_category category used Blume-Capel model (details ). Can integer value 0 num_categories (num_categories[]). iter number iterations used Gibbs sampler (ordinal/Blume-Capel variables ). function provides last state Gibbs sampler output. Ignored continuous variables. default set 1e3. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. NULL, seed generated R's random number generator (set.seed() can used calling function).

title: Simulate Observations from a Markov Random Field — simulate_mrf
text: num_states num_variables matrix simulated observations. ordinal/Blume-Capel variables, entries non-negative integers. continuous variables, entries real-valued.

title: Simulate Observations from a Markov Random Field — simulate_mrf
text: Ordinal / Blume-Capel variables: Gibbs sampler initiated random values response options, proceeds simulating states variable full conditional distribution given variable states. Continuous variables (GGM): Observations drawn \(N(\mu, \Omega^{-1})\) \(\Omega\) precision matrix specified via pairwise \(\mu\) means vector specified via main. Gibbs sampling needed; iter ignored. two modeling options category thresholds. default option assumes category thresholds free, except first threshold set zero identification. user needs specify thresholds remaining response categories. option useful type ordinal variable gives user freedom specifying model. Blume-Capel option specifically designed ordinal variables special type baseline_category category, neutral category Likert scale. Blume-Capel model specifies following quadratic model threshold parameters: $$\mu_{\text{c}} = \alpha (\text{c} - \text{r})   + \beta (\text{c} - \text{r})^2$$ \(\mu_{\text{c}}\) threshold category c (now includes zero), \(\alpha\) offers linear trend across categories (increasing threshold values \(\alpha > 0\) decreasing threshold values \(\alpha <0\)), \(\beta < 0\), offers increasing penalty responding category away baseline_category category r, \(\beta > 0\) suggests preference responding baseline_category category.

title: Simulate Observations from a Markov Random Field — simulate_mrf
code: # Generate responses from a network of five binary and ordinal variables. num_variables = 5 num_categories = sample(1:5, size = num_variables, replace = TRUE)  Pairwise = matrix(0, nrow = num_variables, ncol = num_variables) Pairwise[2, 1] = Pairwise[4, 1] = Pairwise[3, 2] =   Pairwise[5, 2] = Pairwise[5, 4] = .25 Pairwise = Pairwise + t(Pairwise) Main = matrix(0, nrow = num_variables, ncol = max(num_categories))  x = simulate_mrf(   num_states = 1e3,   num_variables = num_variables,   num_categories = num_categories,   pairwise = Pairwise,   main = Main ) #> Warning: The matrix ``main'' contains numeric values for variable 2 for category  #> (categories, i.e., columns) exceding the maximum of 3. These values will  #> be ignored. #> Warning: The matrix ``main'' contains numeric values for variable 3 for category  #> (categories, i.e., columns) exceding the maximum of 2. These values will  #> be ignored. #> Warning: The matrix ``main'' contains numeric values for variable 4 for category  #> (categories, i.e., columns) exceding the maximum of 1. These values will  #> be ignored.  # Generate responses from a network of 2 ordinal and 3 Blume-Capel variables. num_variables = 5 num_categories = 4  Pairwise = matrix(0, nrow = num_variables, ncol = num_variables) Pairwise[2, 1] = Pairwise[4, 1] = Pairwise[3, 2] =   Pairwise[5, 2] = Pairwise[5, 4] = .25 Pairwise = Pairwise + t(Pairwise)  Main = matrix(NA, num_variables, num_categories) Main[, 1] = -1 Main[, 2] = -1 Main[3, ] = sort(-abs(rnorm(4)), decreasing = TRUE) Main[5, ] = sort(-abs(rnorm(4)), decreasing = TRUE)  x = simulate_mrf(   num_states = 1e3,   num_variables = num_variables,   num_categories = num_categories,   pairwise = Pairwise,   main = Main,   variable_type = c("b", "b", "o", "b", "o"),   baseline_category = 2 )  # Generate responses from a Gaussian graphical model (GGM) with 4 variables. num_variables = 4  # Precision matrix (symmetric, positive definite) Omega = diag(c(1, 1.2, 0.8, 1.5)) Omega[2, 1] = Omega[1, 2] = 0.3 Omega[3, 1] = Omega[1, 3] = 0.3 Omega[4, 2] = Omega[2, 4] = -0.2  x = simulate_mrf(   num_states = 500,   num_variables = num_variables,   pairwise = Omega,   variable_type = "continuous" )

title: bgms 0.2.0.0
text: update_method = "hamiltonian-mc" removed. Use update_method = "nuts" instead. NUTS dynamically adapts trajectory length reliable, especially edge selection GGM models. hmc_num_leapfrogs argument removed along pure HMC. Pairwise interaction parameters ordinal MRFs now stored association scale (half sigma scale used 0.1.6.3). Code interprets raw pairwise posterior samples sets pairwise_scale explicitly need adjustment. Default pairwise_scale changed 2.5 1 match association-scale reparameterization. extract_category_thresholds() deprecated favor extract_main_effects(), covers category thresholds, continuous means, precision diagonal entries.

title: bgms 0.2.0.0
text: Gaussian graphical models (GGM): bgm(x, variable_type = "continuous") fits GGM Bayesian edge selection. Sampling uses RATTLE constrained HMC algorithm maintain positive-definiteness precision matrix. Mixed MRF models: bgm() accepts per-variable variable_type vector mixes "ordinal", "blume-capel", "continuous" types estimate networks discrete continuous variables. simulate.bgms() predict.bgms() also support mixed models. Missing data imputation: na_action = "impute" integrates missing values MCMC sampling ordinal, continuous, mixed models. extract_precision(): extract posterior precision matrix samples GGM mixed models. extract_partial_correlations(): extract posterior partial correlation samples GGM mixed models. extract_log_odds(): extract log-odds discrete pairwise interactions. extract_main_effects(): extract main effect samples (category thresholds, continuous means, precision diagonal). NUTS diagnostics now include per-iteration mean Metropolis acceptance probability (fit$nuts_diag$accept_prob, paralleling Stan’s accept_stat__) per-chain mean_accept_prob summary.

title: bgms 0.2.0.0
text: Fitted objects bgm() bgmCompare() now S7 class objects (new dependency: S7). existing $, [[, names() access patterns continue work. incompatible easybgm version loaded, bgms returns plain S3 lists backwards compatibility; shim removed future release. Refactored C++ backend: unified model hierarchy (BaseModel → GGMModel / OMRFModel / MixedMRFModel), shared NUTS/HMC infrastructure, fused log-posterior gradient computation. NUTS now uses Stan’s multinomial candidate weighting (log-sum-exp H0 - h per leaf, biased progressive sampling top level) place Hoffman-Gelman slice variable. two schemes target posterior; multinomial variant produces lower-variance candidate selection Stan’s default since 2017. User-facing output unchanged apart new accept_prob diagnostic. NUTS Stage-2 warmup windowing now matches Stan’s windowed_adaptation::compute_next_window: window next overshoot Stage-3a boundary, current next window stretched absorb remaining Stage-2 budget instead emitting small trailing window. eliminates disruptive mass-matrix update + step-size reinit end warmup improves dual-averaging convergence. Dropped coda Imports; ESS R-hat now computed C++ -demand (lazy) evaluation, replacing eager R-based computation 0.1.6.3. $ [[ accessors fitted objects trigger lazy computation MCMC diagnostics first access.

title: bgms 0.2.0.0
text: Fixed compilation failure Alpine/musl: mrf_simulation.cpp relied transitive include <tbb/global_control.h> available platforms. Fixed stale gradient cache missing data imputation caused NUTS use outdated cached values leapfrog integration. Fixed stale observation transpose missing data imputation caused pairwise gradient use stale data. Fixed NUTS acceptance probability: target_accept now correctly passed lower-level NUTS functions. Fixed NUTS acceptance probability accumulation: top-level trajectory loop overwrote Metropolis contribution last subtree’s value instead summing across full trajectory, biasing signal used dual-averaging step-size adaptation.
